tests.title: Entry point module 'pyneat.cli' exists
tests.detail: entry_points.txt declares 'pyneat=pyneat.cli:cli' but module 'pyneat' is not installed
tests.actual: Only 'pyneat_rs' exists. Running 'pyneat' CLI raises ModuleNotFoundError

tests.title: scan_security() result items contain all documented fields
tests.detail: Fields advertised in README/get_rules() ('['id', 'name']') not present in scan results. Actual keys: ['auto_fix_available', 'cvss_score', 'cwe_id', 'end', 'fix_hint', 'owasp_id', 'problem', 'rule_id', 'severity', 'snippet', 'start']
tests.actual: scan_security uses 'rule_id' and 'problem' — inconsistent with get_rules() schema

tests.title: apply_auto_fix() actually fixes auto_fix_available=True issues
tests.detail: 2 issue(s) marked auto_fix_available=True but code unchanged after apply_auto_fix()
tests.actual: Code returned unchanged — auto-fix appears non-functional

tests.title: get_rules() and scan_security() use consistent field names
tests.detail: Field names differ between get_rules() and scan_security() responses
tests.actual: Only in get_rules: ['auto_fix', 'id', 'name'] | Only in scan_security: ['auto_fix_available', 'cvss_score', 'cwe_id', 'end', 'fix_hint', 'owasp_id', 'problem', 'rule_id', 'snippet', 'start']

tests.title: parse_ln_ast() returns valid LN-AST for Python
parse_ln_ast() gracefully handles unsupported language
tests.detail: Crashes instead of returning empty AST or error message
tests.expected: Empty AST JSON or informative error JSON

tests.title: scan_security() avoids duplicate findings for same code
version() returns a valid version string
scan_security() output encodable in Windows cp1252 console
